project_type: Python Streamlit multi-page data analysis app
test_framework: No formal test framework - validation is via Streamlit import/syntax checks
test_command: python -c "import app; import pages; print('OK')" is not usable due to st.set_page_config. Use: python -c "import utils.data_engine; import utils.visualizations; import utils.supabase_client; import utils.database; print('OK')"
verification_instructions: 1. pip install -r requirements.txt (ensure no errors). 2. python -c to import all new utils modules. 3. Check python -m py_compile on all new files.
environment_constraints: OPEN_INTERNET - full network access. Python 3.11.14 default. No test suite exists - validation via py_compile and import checks.
contribution_requirements: Follow existing patterns: utils/ for pure logic, pages/ for Streamlit UI. Pinned versions in requirements.txt. Supabase features must degrade gracefully. All pages use inject_global_css() and set_page_config with diamond favicon.
key_patterns: Pages follow pattern: set_page_config, inject_global_css, data source selection (built-in/uploaded/online), then feature tabs. Utils are pure logic functions returning tuples or DataFrames. Database functions return (ok, payload) tuples. Session state keys: df (built-in), uploaded_df, online_df, working_df. Supabase graceful degradation via is_configured() checks.
directory_structure: app.py (main entry), pages/ (numbered Streamlit pages 0-12), utils/ (logic modules), data/ (built-in CSV), .streamlit/ (config + secrets template), SUPABASE_SETUP.md (SQL schema guide)
